reference: I have an underactive thyroid and take two hundred milligrams daily of thyroxine. I found lumps on the left side of my neck, and my thyroid is hurting. I had an ultrasound scan, and they found four lumps on the left side and two on the other. They scanned my thyroid, and the results are due on Thursday. However, I have a burning sensation and a significant swelling on the left side above my collarbone. Since having my scan, the stinging is so painful that I have to take painkillers every day. Could this be cancer?
text: I have an underactive thyroid intake of 200mg daily of thyroxine. I found lumps on the left side of my neck, and my thyroid is hurting. I had an ultrasound scan, and they found four lumps on the left side and two on the other. They scan my thyroid, and the results are due on Thursday. However, I have a burning sensation and a significant swelling on the left side above my collar. Since having my scan, the stinging is so painful that I have to take pain killers every day. Is that cancer?
